load in packages

```{.r .cell-code}

library(dplyr)
```

```{.r .cell-code}
d<- iris
```
:::


Q1. Create
Scatter plot of (y) vs (x)
Regression line using geom_smooth(method = "lm")


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
head(d)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width Species
1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2  setosa
2          4.9         3.0          1.4         0.2  setosa
3          4.7         3.2          1.3         0.2  setosa
4          4.6         3.1          1.5         0.2  setosa
5          5.0         3.6          1.4         0.2  setosa
6          5.4         3.9          1.7         0.4  setosa
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=d)+
  aes(x=Petal.Length, y= Petal.Width)+
  geom_point() +
    labs(
    title = "Petal Width vs Petal Length",
    x = "Petal Length", y = "Petal Width")+
  geom_smooth() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col= "red")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Homework_09_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Q2. Create
Boxplot or violin plot
Points overlaid using jitter (geom_jitter())



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Petal.Length, fill = Species)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Homework_09_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Q3. Create 
Jittered points
Logistic regression curve using geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"))


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
d2<- filter(iris, Species!="setosa")
library(ggplot2)

d3 <- d2 %>%
  mutate(binary = if_else(Species == "virginica", 1, 0))
head(d3)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width    Species binary
1          7.0         3.2          4.7         1.4 versicolor      0
2          6.4         3.2          4.5         1.5 versicolor      0
3          6.9         3.1          4.9         1.5 versicolor      0
4          5.5         2.3          4.0         1.3 versicolor      0
5          6.5         2.8          4.6         1.5 versicolor      0
6          5.7         2.8          4.5         1.3 versicolor      0
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(d3, aes(x = Petal.Length, y = binary)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", 
              method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Logistic Regression with Jittered Data",
       x = "Petal Length)",
       y = "Species (Versicolor=0, Virginica=1)") 
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Homework_09_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Q4. Create a mosaic plot using two discrete variables


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
d4 <- d3 %>%
  mutate(binarypetallength = if_else(Petal.Length < "5.5", 1, 0))
head(d4)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width    Species binary
1          7.0         3.2          4.7         1.4 versicolor      0
2          6.4         3.2          4.5         1.5 versicolor      0
3          6.9         3.1          4.9         1.5 versicolor      0
4          5.5         2.3          4.0         1.3 versicolor      0
5          6.5         2.8          4.6         1.5 versicolor      0
6          5.7         2.8          4.5         1.3 versicolor      0
  binarypetallength
1                 1
2                 1
3                 1
4                 1
5                 1
6                 1
```
